message:
feat(core): Add TestClient for in-process HTTP testing

Introduce comprehensive testing utilities for fastapi applications that
enable fast, in-process testing without network overhead.

Key components:

- TestClient: Main testing interface wrapping any Handler
  - HTTP-like API (get, post, put, delete, patch, options, head)
  - Thread-safe design with Arc-wrapped handler and mutex-protected cookies
  - Deterministic testing support via configurable seed for Lab runtime
  - Request ID counter for tracing/debugging test sequences

- RequestBuilder: Fluent API for constructing test requests
  - Query parameter support with automatic URL encoding
  - Header configuration (bytes and string variants)
  - Body setting (raw bytes, string, and JSON with auto Content-Type)
  - Per-request cookie support separate from client jar

- TestResponse: Response wrapper with assertion helpers
  - Status code accessors and category checks (success, redirect, 4xx, 5xx)
  - Header accessors (case-insensitive lookup)
  - Body extraction (bytes, text, JSON deserialization)
  - Chainable assertion methods for readable test code

- CookieJar: Session persistence across requests
  - Automatic parsing of Set-Cookie headers from responses
  - Automatic injection of Cookie header into requests
  - Simple name-value storage with CRUD operations

Dependencies:

- Added futures-executor crate for block_on() to execute async
  handlers synchronously in test contexts
- Minimal dependency footprint following project principles

Integration:

- Uses Cx::for_testing() for asupersync integration
- Creates RequestContext with incrementing request IDs
- Full Handler trait compatibility for testing any handler type

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-22x","title":"Root path handling for reverse proxy","description":"Support root_path for ASGI apps behind reverse proxy. Configuration: root_path string (default empty), root_path_in_servers bool (add to OpenAPI servers). Affects: URL generation, OpenAPI server list, request.url_for(). Example: app behind /api/v1 proxy should have root_path=/api/v1. Test: verify OpenAPI servers include root_path, generated URLs correct, works with path operations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:39.163771266-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:39.163771266-05:00","labels":["app","config","phase-2","proxy"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-28p","title":"Debug mode with enhanced error context","description":"Implement debug vs production mode for error responses. Production mode hides internal details, returns only user-safe error messages, no stack traces or file paths. Debug mode (opt-in) includes source location (file:line:function) in error responses, shows full validation error context, includes handler name and route pattern. Configuration via FastAPI::new().debug(true).debug_header(X-Debug-Token, secret). Test requirements: verify debug info hidden in production, shown when enabled, debug header auth works, no sensitive data leaks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:26:57.049583465-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:26:57.049583465-05:00","labels":["developer-experience","errors","phase-1","ux"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2dh","title":"Route priority and ordering rules","description":"Define clear route matching priority:
1. Exact/static segments over parameters
2. Named params over wildcards
3. Earlier registration order as tiebreaker
- Document rules clearly
- Consistent with FastAPI behavior

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- /users/me matches before /users/{id}
- /files/{name} matches before /files/{*path}
- Priority documented in user guide
- No ambiguous matching

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Priority scenarios
- Unit: Tiebreaker ordering
- Integration: Complex route set","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:41:17.615602723-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:41:17.615602723-05:00","labels":["phase-2","priority","routing"]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2h0","title":"Response type checking for OpenAPI","description":"Ensure response types match OpenAPI declarations. When using #[response(200, UserResponse)], verify handler actually returns UserResponse or impl IntoResponse<Body=UserResponse>. Detect mismatches at compile time. Implementation: proc macro that checks return type against declared response schemas. Test: verify compile fails when return type mismatches declared response, succeeds when matched.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:28:00.658022193-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:28:00.658022193-05:00","labels":["compile-time","openapi","response","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2h0","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-ams","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:29:03.753221476-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2i0","title":"SecurityScopes auto-injection","description":"Implement SecurityScopes for scope checking:

STRUCTURE:
SecurityScopes {
    scopes: Vec<String>,     // All required scopes from dependency chain
    scope_str: String,       // scopes.join(' ')
}

AUTO-INJECTION:
When handler parameter is typed as SecurityScopes,
inject with aggregated scopes from:
1. Route-level Security dependencies
2. Parent dependencies with scopes
3. Preserving order

USAGE:
async fn get_admin(
    token: BearerToken,
    scopes: SecurityScopes,
) -> impl IntoResponse {
    // scopes.scopes = ['admin', 'users:read']
    // Verify token has required scopes
}

SCOPE AGGREGATION:
- Collect from parent dependencies
- own_scopes + parent_scopes
- Deduplicate while preserving order

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scopes aggregated correctly
- Order preserved
- Works with nested Security deps
- scope_str formatted correctly

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Scope aggregation
- Unit: Order preservation
- Unit: Nested dependencies
- Integration: Full auth flow","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:28.923582672-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:28.923582672-05:00","labels":["oauth2","phase-6","scopes","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2i0","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-nij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:42.697815751-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-2im","title":"Timing-safe comparison for auth tokens","description":"Provide constant-time comparison functions for sensitive values to prevent timing attacks on authentication.

Implementation:
- constant_time_compare() function
- Wrap subtle crate or implement carefully
- Use for password/token/HMAC comparison
- Document timing attack risks
- Integration with auth extractors

Acceptance Criteria:
- Comparison time independent of string content
- Works for any byte slices
- Clear documentation on usage
- Integration with token validators

Tests:
- Same strings compare equal
- Different strings compare unequal
- Timing measurement shows constant time
- Different length strings handled safely","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:28.030757625-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:28.030757625-05:00","labels":["auth","crypto","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-2im","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-3d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:36.236605507-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-6c2","title":"Json body extractor implementation","description":"Implement Json<T> extractor:
- Validate Content-Type: application/json
- Configurable body size limit
- Deserialize JSON to struct
- Detailed error messages for parse failures
- Integration with asupersync checkpoints

DEPENDS ON: Body handling

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Json<T> deserializes request body
- Wrong Content-Type = 415
- Oversized body = 413
- Parse error = 422 with details

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Successful deserialization
- Unit: Content-Type validation
- Unit: Size limit enforcement
- Unit: Error message quality
- Security: Large body handling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:42:45.289220636-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:42:45.289220636-05:00","labels":["extractors","json","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-6c2","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-2l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:47:08.930781784-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-6jg","title":"Additional response types (Redirect, File, HTML)","description":"Implement specialized response types:

REDIRECT RESPONSE:
- RedirectResponse::temporary(url) -> 307
- RedirectResponse::permanent(url) -> 308
- RedirectResponse::see_other(url) -> 303
- Sets Location header

FILE RESPONSE:
- FileResponse::new(path)
- Sets Content-Type from extension
- Sets Content-Disposition for downloads
- Optional: Range request support
- Streams file without full buffering

HTML RESPONSE:
- HtmlResponse::new(html_string)
- Sets Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8

PLAIN TEXT:
- TextResponse::new(text)
- Sets Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

EMPTY RESPONSE:
- NoContent::new() -> 204
- For DELETE success, etc.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Each response type sets correct headers
- File streaming works for large files
- Redirect preserves query string
- Content-Type inference for files

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each response type
- Unit: Header correctness
- Integration: Browser redirect following
- Integration: File download","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:24.174586945-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:24.174586945-05:00","labels":["http","phase-2","response"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-6jg","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-0qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:44.206281873-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-6w9","title":"Integration test framework for multi-component tests","description":"Framework for testing multiple components working together. Goes beyond unit tests to verify component interactions.

Implementation:
- Test harness that boots full app
- Database fixtures support
- State reset between tests
- Parallel test isolation
- Shared test resources

Test Scenarios:
- Full request lifecycle (parser -> router -> handler -> response)
- Auth flow (login -> get token -> use token)
- Middleware chain execution
- Error propagation through layers

Acceptance Criteria:
- Full app can be tested end-to-end
- Tests are isolated from each other
- State resets reliably between tests
- Performance is acceptable (not too slow)
- Integration with cargo test

Tests:
- Integration test runs successfully
- Tests dont interfere with each other
- Cleanup runs even on failure
- Parallel tests work correctly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:16:14.914514024-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:16:14.914514024-05:00","labels":["infrastructure","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-6w9","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-86q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:16:35.347259757-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-6w9","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-noa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:16:35.388099766-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-7f1","title":"Request inspection middleware for development","description":"Development middleware that logs detailed request/response info. Features: log full request details (headers, body preview, timing), log response status/headers/body preview, configurable verbosity levels, pretty-print JSON bodies, highlight slow requests. Output format: --> POST /api/users with headers and body, <-- 201 Created (12ms) with response. Test: verify all components logged, body truncation, slow request highlighting, sensitive header filtering.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:26:57.904843307-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:26:57.904843307-05:00","labels":["debugging","developer-experience","middleware","ux"]}

- AppBuilder with fluent API
- Route registration
- Middleware registration (with order)
- State registration
- Configuration injection
- Build into runnable App

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Clean builder API
- Compile-time route registration
- Middleware ordering explicit
- State type-safe
- Build validates completeness

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Builder methods
- Integration: Complete app build
- Compile-fail: Invalid configurations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:43:50.379675971-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:43:50.379675971-05:00","labels":["app","builder","phase-2"]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-7xf","title":"Request ID middleware","description":"Implement request ID handling:
- Generate unique request ID per request
- Accept X-Request-ID from client (optional)
- Add X-Request-ID to response
- Propagate to logging context
- UUID or shorter ID format

DEPENDS ON: Middleware trait, Logging

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every request gets unique ID
- ID in request and response headers
- ID available in handlers
- ID in all log lines

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: ID generation uniqueness
- Unit: Client ID acceptance
- Integration: ID propagation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:43:30.058605819-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:43:30.058605819-05:00","labels":["logging","middleware","phase-2","requestid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-7xf","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:47:10.607058219-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Headers: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options (DENY/SAMEORIGIN), X-XSS-Protection, Content-Security-Policy (configurable), Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
